perturbation.original_text: 5.1. All information disclosed to the Investor by the Company in any form shall be and remain the property of the Company. Documents and any copies of them, abstracts, memoranda, or other documentation shall be immediately returned to the Company or destroyed at the written request of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1. All information disclosed to the Investor by the Company in any form shall be considered jointly owned by both the Company and the Investor. The Investor is not obligated to return or destroy any documents, copies, abstracts, or memoranda unless mutually agreed upon in writing.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause by changing ownership of the information and removing the obligation to return or destroy it, creating uncertainty about the company's control over its proprietary information and contradicts the definition of Investor's obligations in 3.4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.4. The Investor is obliged to keep, withhold, and hold any conﬁdential information, take measures, which are greater than or equal to measures which the Investor takes to protect his or her own conﬁdential information, to secure the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in 5.1 modifies the ownership of disclosed information from the Company's property to jointly owned. It also removes the Investor's obligation to return or destroy documents. This directly contradicts 3.4, which obliges the Investor to keep confidential information secure and take measures to protect the Proprietary Information. By changing the ownership and removing the obligation to return documents, the modified text creates a strong contradiction, making it unclear how the Investor is obliged to protect the Company's information.

perturbation.original_text: 7.1. The Agreement shall enter into force as soon as it has been accepted by the Investor and also shall be effective during 5 (ﬁve) years from the termination of the effective period of the Offer, so that the Investor shall fulﬁll its obligations of conﬁdentiality for 5 (ﬁve) years following the expiration of the Agreement or Offer.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.1. This Agreement shall enter into force upon acceptance by the Investor and shall continue indefinitely, unless terminated earlier by the Investor. The Investor's confidentiality obligations shall cease immediately upon termination of this Agreement by the Investor.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly defined a 5-year confidentiality period. This change allows the investor to terminate the agreement at any time, immediately ending confidentiality obligations. This contradicts the company's expectation of a fixed confidentiality period and undermines the purpose of the NDA, contradicting the general purpose of section 3, where there are confidentiality restructions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Conﬁdentiality restrictions2.1. Obligations of parties to secure the Proprietary Information under the terms indicated in the Agreement constitute the subject of the agreement. 3.1. The Investor is authorized to grant access to the proprietary information only to those parties who require access to the proprietary information in realizing the investors’ rights under the Offer in order to achieve the provision of the Proprietary Information, are obliged not to disclose the Proprietary Information and informed about the fact of the conclusion of the Agreement. The Investor is obliged to provide the list of Investor’s Agents who have received access to the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 7.1 allows the investor to terminate the agreement at any time and immediately end confidentiality obligations. This directly contradicts the original intent and implications of Section 3, which outlines the confidentiality restrictions and obligations on the investor, suggesting a continuous obligation related to proprietary information. The changed text therefore creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.6. The Investor shall be entitled to make copies, abstracts, memoranda, or other documents (including documents made by mechanical or electronic means) related to the conﬁdential information, which shall also be marked as «Commercial Secret» and/or «Conﬁdential» only to the extents that are necessary for the conﬁdential information achievement. The Investor shall provide the true recording of all copies and places where the duplicates copies of the conﬁdential information are kept, and access limitation to the speciﬁed copies, abstracts, memoranda, and other documents to third-parties except for his or her authorized Agents, according to the section 3.1. of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.6. The Investor shall be entitled to make unlimited copies, abstracts, memoranda, or other documents (including documents made by mechanical or electronic means) related to the conﬁdential information. The Investor is not required to mark such copies as «Commercial Secret» and/or «Conﬁdential». The Investor is not required to keep any record of copies or their locations and is not required to limit access to these copies.
perturbation.explanation: This change allows the Investor to make unlimited, unmarked copies of confidential information, with no restrictions on access or record-keeping. This directly contradicts the security and confidentiality provisions of the original agreement by removing all controls over the spread of proprietary information and making the agreement ambigous to the protection of the proprietary information. This contradicts the initial definition of Agent of Investor, where there is an authorized agent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3. An Agent of the Investor is an authorized person of the investor who has access to the Proprietary Information. The Proprietary Information is any information of the Company marked as «Commercial Secret» or «Conﬁdential» with actual or potential commercial value by virtue of the fact third-parties are unfamiliar with it, submitted to the Investor by the Company in written form by documents transmission, including the electronic transmission of documents secured from unauthorized access to the transmitted information, the information related to business or ﬁnance plans and strategies including, without limitation information about markets, ﬁnancial documents, ﬁnancial statements and accounting (except for cases deﬁned by law), contractual relationships, pricing and marketing of goods (works and services), technical information, commercial secrets, know-how, research, production schedules, concepts, intellectual activity property (which includes discoveries, inventions, rationalization proposals, utility models, constructions, industrial models that are non-proprietary for some reasons, computer software, databases, logotypes scratches that are non-registered for some reasons).
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3.6 allows the Investor to make unlimited, unmarked copies of confidential information without any restrictions on access or record-keeping. This directly contradicts Section 1.3, which defines 'Agent of the Investor' as an authorized person with access to 'Proprietary Information,' which is explicitly marked as 'Commercial Secret' or 'Confidential.' The removal of the marking and access control requirements in the changed text creates a strong contradiction, as it undermines the very definition and protection of Proprietary Information, making the concept of an 'Agent of the Investor' in Section 1.3 potentially meaningless in the context of these unmarked and uncontrolled copies. The original text explicitly limits access to authorized agents whereas the Changed text has no such requirement.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2. The Company reserves the right to conduct the analysis of the conﬁdential information protection measures taken by the Investor. Should the denial to provide the information about measures taken for the conﬁdential information protection, received by the Investor, or detection of insufﬁcient measures taken to protect the conﬁdential information, the company is authorized to deny entrusting the conﬁdential information or refuse to fulﬁll the obligations of any agreements concluded between the parties unilaterally, according to which the Investor shall procure or procured accesses to the conﬁdential information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2. The Company has no right to monitor or analyze the confidential information protection measures taken by the Investor. The Investor is under no obligation to disclose any information about their security measures. The Company is not authorized to take any action based on the Investor's security measures.
perturbation.explanation: This clause strips the Company of its right to monitor the Investor's security practices and take action if those practices are insufficient. This directly contradicts the overall intent of the agreement to protect proprietary information and contradicts section 3.4, where the Investor is obliged to secure the proprietary information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.4. The Investor is obliged to keep, withhold, and hold any conﬁdential information, take measures, which are greater than or equal to measures which the Investor takes to protect his or her own conﬁdential information, to secure the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 5.2 explicitly states that the Company has no right to monitor the Investor's security measures and the Investor is under no obligation to disclose security information. This directly contradicts section 3.4, which obliges the Investor to take measures to secure proprietary information, including measures equal to or greater than those used to protect their own confidential information. The change fundamentally removes the Company's ability to ensure compliance with the security obligations outlined in section 3.4, therefore creating a strong in-text contradiction.
